ardy_ledger.claim_boundary: No ARDY candidate is admitted, no large candidate artifact is published acceptance evidence, and no readability or runtime gate passes.

iterations.additional_failures: Strike and Block remained overhead-convergent; selected runtime-camera renders also cropped the weapon.
Dense world-space constraints on both feet conflicted with moving-root Strike and Grab plans; Block improved continuity but still exceeded 20 mm.

block.prompt: Plant the feet in a fighting stance, lift both forearms and both hands together across the upper chest and face in a compact high defensive guard, brace against an incoming overhead strike, then recover.

grab.prompt: From a low fighting stance, step forward toward an opponent, extend both open hands together at chest height, close the distance into a two-handed torso grab, pull backward, release, and recover.

strike.prompt: Plant the feet in a fighting stance, raise both hands together above the right shoulder as if gripping one weapon, drive both hands forward and downward in one decisive overhead strike, then recover to guard.

remaining_gates: at least 20 blinded human participants with >=80% accuracy for every action
source-hash verifier wired into the repository release gate

candidate_visual_mrt_receipt.claim_boundary: Candidate-scope diagnostic receipt only. It is not motion admission, human readability evidence, or live-runtime playback evidence.

historical_revision_isolation.do_not_merge_rule: Each row is a different evaluated revision/run. Metrics and hashes are preserved independently; no favorable composite is computed.

rows.revision_binding: index/capture JSON does not embed git revision; directory suffix and matching public artifact name are contextual provenance only

rows.reason: No exact-HEAD PVP-005 Visual Harness or Permanent Adversarial Visual Contract run exists; no current capture is claimed.
rows.revision_binding: git HEAD/tree and public remote exact head

architecture_decision.safe_implementable_next_atomic_unit: define and test canonical NeuralPlanPacket bytes, payload SHA-256, truth bindings, and an asynchronous fail-closed buffer without invoking or relabelling the released checkpoint as feedback authority

motionbricks_admission.exact_blocker: No admitted compatible MotionBricks checkpoint exposes the interaction/physics-feedback conditioning channel required by the live high-frequency layer.

current_fail_closed_findings: The committed visual harness does not satisfy the 16-view layered PVP-005 contract.
No genuine first-six-frame blinded human judgments exist.
The live sword uses an independent camera-relative transform.
content.evidence_policy: Historical reports retain their evaluated revisions. New PVP-005 evidence must name an exact reachable source revision and must not combine outputs from different source revisions.

missing_expected_inventory: native first-person gameplay video >=1920x1080 with declared self-body crop policy
eight explicitly named and semantically distinct phase anchors
blinded human acceptance packet and owner acceptance decision
evidence_inventory.scope: all submitted historical PVP-005 images/AOVs/diagnostic contact sheets and receipts under tracked candidate/report evidence, plus every owner validation_evidence file (media, index/capture, logs, exit receipts, and checksum receipts)
evidence_inventory.static_png_note: Every PNG has encoded_frame_count=1. Semantic panel_count records contact-sheet/strip layout; panels are not encoded video frames.

Candidate screener emits v2 while packet builder accepts only v1.

github_remote_state.query_transport: unauthenticated public GitHub REST API v2022-11-28 (gh CLI installed but no account authenticated)

steps.name: Render every current candidate and fail on visual gates
Verify pinned DCC and runtime carrier
Validate reachable revision and complete provenance

algorithm.object_id: Split source sheets/strips into 512x512 panels; count exact RGBA actor=(255,0,0,255), weapon=(0,255,0,255), and border-union pixels at 8 px and 26 px. Compare per-sample counts to capture.json.
algorithm.socket: Aggregate receipt pose_metrics min/max and threshold crossings only. No 3D distance was derived from pixels and no capture/render binary was rerun.
algorithm.temporal: SHA-256 raw RGBA bytes for each of eight strip panels and each full orbit sheet over f00-f07; enumerate exact duplicates. Compute adjacent changed-pixel fraction by per-channel difference union.
algorithm.timestamps: Compare receipt simulation/presentation timestamps to exact index/60 seconds for indices 0..7.

limitations: No angle-error series exists in owner capture receipts, so the <=3 degree active-grip gate cannot be recomputed.
No 3D joint/socket source was executed because rerunning the capture/render harness was prohibited.
No temporal video duration exists; PNG encoded frame_count is 1 and panel_count is semantic layout.

direct_object_id_pixel_recomputation.method: Pillow decoded source PNG object-ID pixels; exact RGBA actor=(255,0,0,255), weapon=(0,255,0,255); 8-pixel tile-edge margin mirrors src/bin/pvp005_capture.rs:id_metrics

receipt_pose_and_timestamp_aggregation.method: read-only aggregation of receipt pose/timestamp arrays; no 3D socket distance was re-derived because doing so requires rerunning the render/capture harness, which this task prohibits

temporal_panel_analysis.encoded_media_caveat: Each strip is one still PNG with eight semantic panels, not an encoded eight-frame video.
temporal_panel_analysis.method: decoded 512x512 panels for 8-panel PNG strips; exact raw RGBA hashes plus consecutive changed-pixel fractions; orbit frame duplicates checked by source-file SHA256 per channel/background

pixel_inspection.method: vision_analyze on actual full-resolution source paths; no thumbnails, OCR-only, filename-only, or telemetry-only substitution

pixel_observations.actor_weapon_contact: One isolated full-body actor; no opponent or contact event. A very dark sword enters from the top and meets the raised hands, but its upper extent is outside the panels.
pixel_observations.background_contrast: Bronze actor is clear on charcoal; the dark sword has poor charcoal contrast.
pixel_observations.crop_safe_margin: Actor body fits, but the sword intersects the top image boundary throughout; complete-weapon safe margin is not present.
pixel_observations.first_person_tells: None. This is an isolated full-body diagnostic strip, not native player-eye gameplay.
pixel_observations.overlays: No diagnostic overlay is visibly present.
pixel_observations.phase_distinction: Eight panels change slightly, but show no opponent, impact, recovery, or explicit action-phase labels.
pixel_observations.reference_pose_or_deformation: Lower body and root remain visually near-static while the raised arms change modestly. No gross mesh tearing is visible; pixels alone cannot identify bind matrices.
pixel_observations.two_hand_grip: Both hands converge overhead, but the strip has no socket overlay and cannot prove a coherent two-hand grip.

samples.selection_reason: Tracked eight-panel source strip named by the immutable candidate manifest.

pixel_observations.actor_weapon_contact: One isolated full-body actor; no opponent or incoming contact. Arms rise from waist/chest toward overhead; the dark sword becomes clearer later and enters from the upper left.
pixel_observations.background_contrast: Bronze actor is clear; dark sword is low contrast against charcoal.
pixel_observations.crop_safe_margin: Actor fits with large body margin; the sword is clipped at the upper-left boundary in later panels.
pixel_observations.first_person_tells: None. Isolated full-body diagnostic media only.
pixel_observations.overlays: No diagnostic overlay is visibly present.
pixel_observations.phase_distinction: Visible arm lift across eight panels, but no incoming strike, brace, contact, or explicit phase anchor; late pose converges with the overhead Strike silhouette.
pixel_observations.reference_pose_or_deformation: Legs and torso stay rigidly upright while arms lift. No gross mesh tearing is visible; pixels alone cannot identify bind matrices.
pixel_observations.two_hand_grip: Hands approach each other, but there is no socket overlay and the sequence does not prove a compact coherent two-hand high guard.

samples.selection_reason: Tracked eight-panel source strip named by the immutable candidate manifest.

pixel_observations.actor_weapon_contact: One isolated full-body actor and no opponent/contact. No sword is visibly present. Arms begin asymmetric and finish together overhead, not together at an opponent torso.
pixel_observations.background_contrast: Bronze actor remains legible on charcoal.
pixel_observations.crop_safe_margin: Visible actor fits with large margin; no weapon is present to verify complete-weapon framing.
pixel_observations.first_person_tells: None. Isolated full-body diagnostic media only.
pixel_observations.overlays: No diagnostic overlay is visibly present.
pixel_observations.phase_distinction: Eight panels change, but the dominant motion is arms rising toward an overhead pose; no approach, torso contact, pull, release, or phase labels are visible.
pixel_observations.reference_pose_or_deformation: Lower body remains near-static and upright. No gross mesh tearing is visible; pixels alone cannot identify bind matrices.
pixel_observations.two_hand_grip: The image cannot support an unarmed two-hand torso grab: hands do not close together at chest-level contact with a target.

samples.selection_reason: Tracked eight-panel source strip named by the immutable candidate manifest.

pixel_observations.actor_weapon_contact: A distant, isolated full-body actor holds a long sword overhead; no opponent or contact is visible.
pixel_observations.background_contrast: Offwhite makes the dark sword much clearer; charcoal makes the bronze actor clear but the sword low contrast. Cropping persists on both.
pixel_observations.crop_safe_margin: All 16 background/frame samples have nonzero pixels inside the historical 8 px crop border; sword repeatedly reaches a tile boundary.
pixel_observations.first_person_tells: No self body, player hands, environment, opponent interaction, HUD, or camera-motion cue. The image is visually indistinguishable from a full-body diagnostic camera, so the label does not prove native player-eye capture.
pixel_observations.independent_sword: Sword/hand overlap is visible, but pose-derived ownership cannot be proven from the beauty strip.
pixel_observations.overlays: Beauty images contain no visible socket/path/contact/truth overlay.
pixel_observations.phase_distinction: No exact duplicate panels; only 3.28%-3.38% of charcoal beauty pixels change between adjacent panels. The eight timestamps are Reveal samples, not eight visibly named action phases.
pixel_observations.reference_pose_or_deformation: Torso and legs remain almost static while arms/sword change modestly. No gross mesh tearing; bind-pose truth is not recoverable from pixels.
pixel_observations.two_hand_grip: Hands cluster around the hilt, but beauty pixels do not prove both 3D sockets are coherent.

samples.selection_reason: Both paired-background, eight-panel images carrying the first_person label.

pixel_observations.actor_weapon_contact: A distant isolated full-body actor holds a sword across waist/lower chest; no incoming strike, opponent, or contact is visible.
pixel_observations.background_contrast: Offwhite strongly improves dark-sword contrast; actor remains legible. Charcoal suppresses sword contrast.
pixel_observations.crop_safe_margin: All 16 samples fail the historical 8 px crop border; sword extends to or beyond a tile boundary.
pixel_observations.first_person_tells: No native player-eye tells; this presents as isolated full-body diagnostics.
pixel_observations.independent_sword: Sword/hand overlap exists but pose-derived ownership is not proven.
pixel_observations.overlays: Beauty images contain no visible socket/path/contact/truth overlay.
pixel_observations.phase_distinction: No exact duplicate panels; 3.14%-3.29% of charcoal beauty pixels change per adjacent panel. No compact high-guard brace/contact/recovery phases are visually separable.
pixel_observations.reference_pose_or_deformation: Upright body and legs remain nearly static. No gross mesh tear; bind-pose truth is not recoverable from pixels.
pixel_observations.two_hand_grip: One hand is near the hilt while the second-hand relation is ambiguous at this scale; no coherent two-hand socket claim is supported by beauty pixels.

samples.selection_reason: Both paired-background, eight-panel images carrying the first_person label.

pixel_observations.actor_weapon_contact: A sword is plainly visible through the sequence, often very large and crossing the actor; no opponent or torso contact exists.
pixel_observations.background_contrast: Offwhite makes the blade unmistakable; bronze actor stays legible. Charcoal blade contrast is poorer.
pixel_observations.crop_safe_margin: All 16 first-person samples fail the 8 px border; large blade portions leave the frame.
pixel_observations.first_person_tells: No native player-eye cues; isolated full-body diagnostic presentation.
pixel_observations.independent_sword: Weapon sweeps across and away from the hands in ways that require socket AOV/3D data to diagnose; beauty pixels alone cannot establish transform authority.
pixel_observations.overlays: Beauty images contain no visible diagnostic overlay.
pixel_observations.phase_distinction: No exact duplicates; adjacent charcoal beauty change is 5.03%-6.69%, dominated visibly by the sword. No approach/contact/pull/release phases are established.
pixel_observations.reference_pose_or_deformation: Lower body remains upright while arms and sword move. No gross mesh tear; bind-pose truth is not recoverable from pixels.
pixel_observations.two_hand_grip: Arms are separated and do not read as an unarmed two-hand torso grab. The pixels contradict the historical manifest field weapon_in_reveal=false.

samples.selection_reason: Both paired-background, eight-panel images carrying the first_person label; chosen to test the historical weapon_in_reveal=false declaration.

pixel_observations.actor_weapon_contact: Overhead sword pose in all orbit panels; no opponent/contact. Complete sword is clipped in many views.
pixel_observations.background_contrast: Bronze actor clear; dark sword weak on charcoal.
pixel_observations.crop_safe_margin: Weapon crosses tile boundaries in problematic views; historical object-ID crop failures are mechanically confirmed.
pixel_observations.independent_sword: AOV indicates hand/weapon alignment disagreement; 2D views cannot establish the transform implementation.
pixel_observations.overlays: Beauty has no overlay. The socket AOV visibly contains colored markers/connectors around hands and hilt; it cannot establish 3D meters without receipt geometry.
pixel_observations.reference_pose_or_deformation: Neutral upright lower body with raised arms; no gross mesh tear. This does not itself prove bind matrices.
pixel_observations.two_hand_grip: Colored hand/socket markers show a visibly separated secondary pair in multiple projections, consistent with a receipt error but not a pixel-derived 3D distance.

pixel_observations.actor_weapon_contact: Arms point apart and a sword is visible in most views; no opponent or two-hand torso contact. Weapon disappears in the two receipt-identified projections.

pixel_observations.crop_safe_margin: Weapon is cropped/disappears in views; object-ID recomputation confirms six zero-weapon orbit samples across paired backgrounds/frames.
pixel_observations.independent_sword: Sword/hand relation varies strongly by view; pixels do not establish 3D authority.
pixel_observations.overlays: Socket AOV shows colored markers/connectors; this depicts 2D projections, not independently recomputed 3D socket truth.
pixel_observations.reference_pose_or_deformation: Upright actor and no gross mesh tear.
pixel_observations.two_hand_grip: Does not read as the historical unarmed two-hand grab. Under the newer W0 contract the left hand is intentionally inactive, so its large distance must not be misreported as an active-grip failure.

red_gates.likely_cause_inference: No continuous packaged-build capture and provenance manifest have been accepted/published; this is consistent with the deliberate fail-closed README.
red_gates.observed_fact: verify_latest_media exits 1 and README explicitly says no valid canonical gameplay media is present.
red_gates.prohibited_fallback: Do not substitute autoplay, isolated screenshots, diagnostic strips, edited sequences, or another revision.
red_gates.smallest_future_falsifying_experiment: Capture one continuous native packaged-build session, hash the build/video/overview/truth, create the required manifest, and rerun python3 tools/verify_latest_media.py at the same revision.

expected_non_repository_outputs: GitHub Actions run records and revision-bound visual artifacts for exact HEAD
red_gates.likely_cause_inference: The current docs-only head did not match either visual workflow path filter, while the previous relevant commit exercised and failed the render/admission gates.
red_gates.observed_fact: Exact HEAD has only two successful CI verify runs. Required visual/adversarial runs are absent; latest branch runs both failed.
red_gates.prohibited_fallback: Do not treat generic CI success, a different SHA, skipped jobs, or config-only validators as visual green.
red_gates.smallest_future_falsifying_experiment: workflow_dispatch both required workflows at exact HEAD and require every required job/check to complete success with revision-bound artifacts.

red_gates.likely_cause_inference: No revision-bound blinded first-six-frame trial meeting n>=20/action, >=0.8 accuracy/action, and <=0.2 pairwise confusion has been completed and accepted.
red_gates.observed_fact: Candidate manifest is pending_blinded_human_trials with runtime_promoted=false; both motion and provenance acceptance validators exit 1 because the acceptance packet is absent. PR review state is COMMENTED, not APPROVED.
red_gates.prohibited_fallback: No simulated reviewer, LLM opinion, filename label, telemetry, or owner-approval inference.
red_gates.smallest_future_falsifying_experiment: Run a ForgeLens-bound blinded raw trial for Strike/Block/Grab, preserve judgments and capture hashes, then create a pass packet only if every threshold passes.

red_gates.likely_cause_inference: The harness rendered diagnostic duel-camera panels rather than recording native packaged gameplay with declared self-body framing.
red_gates.observed_fact: No video exists in submitted inventory. All first_person-labelled files are single PNG strips of eight 512x512 panels and visually show a distant isolated full-body actor without native player-eye tells.
red_gates.prohibited_fallback: Do not rename a contact sheet, orbit strip, autoplay capture, or isolated actor render as gameplay.
red_gates.smallest_future_falsifying_experiment: Record unedited native first-person gameplay at >=1920x1080, declare the self-body crop policy, and show opponent interaction plus truth-bound capture provenance.

expected_non_repository_outputs: new revision-bound object-ID sheets and capture.json receipts
red_gates.likely_cause_inference: Fixed camera radius/FOV and action/weapon envelopes do not keep the complete weapon inside frame; Grab also becomes edge-on or leaves frame.
red_gates.observed_fact: Direct object-ID pixel recomputation exactly matches receipts: Strike 144/256, Block 126/256, Grab 144/256 orbit failures at the 8 px border; every action has 16/16 first-person crop failures. At a 5% (26 px) border, failures are 160, 128, and 152 orbit samples respectively and 16/16 first-person samples for every action.
red_gates.prohibited_fallback: No auto-fit camera, hidden weapon, crop masking, downsample concealment, or metric threshold relaxation.
red_gates.smallest_future_falsifying_experiment: Render the same required frames/views with the locked camera and reject any nonzero actor/weapon object-ID pixel in the 5% border before human review.

expected_non_repository_outputs: new Strike/Block capture.json pose metrics and hand_socket_alignment AOVs
red_gates.likely_cause_inference: Weapon pose is anchored to the primary hand/trajectory without solving the secondary hand to the second grip socket.
red_gates.observed_fact: Current W0 requires two active hand sockets for Strike/Block with <=0.01 m and <=3 degrees. Owner receipt aggregation records Strike 0.02283432-0.090210155 m and Block 0.23075762-0.24451943 m left-grip error; every sampled frame exceeds 0.01 m. Socket AOVs visibly project secondary-marker offsets.
red_gates.prohibited_fallback: Do not infer 3D socket success from 2D overlap or deactivate the second hand to pass a two-hand policy.
red_gates.smallest_future_falsifying_experiment: At all eight 60 Hz frames, recompute both hand-to-socket 3D position/angle errors from pose-derived weapon transforms and require every active socket to pass before rendering.

expected_non_repository_outputs: new Grab capture.json, beauty/object-ID/socket/contact AOVs, and review packet
red_gates.likely_cause_inference: Historical capture combined a sword-bearing renderer with an unarmed candidate semantic, then the later contract changed Grab ownership without a new admitted capture.
red_gates.observed_fact: Historical manifest says weapon_in_reveal=false and prompts an unarmed two-hand torso grab, while owner pixels visibly contain a large sword and no opponent contact. Newer W0 instead requires one-hand-right visible stow. Grab has six zero-weapon orbit samples in owner object-ID receipts.
red_gates.prohibited_fallback: Do not count cropping/disappearance as stow, merge historical unarmed labels with the newer one-hand policy, or call a sword sweep a torso grab.
red_gates.smallest_future_falsifying_experiment: Freeze one revision-bound Grab policy, render all required views, and prove its exact hand roles, opponent contact semantics, stow zone, and complete weapon visibility.

red_gates.likely_cause_inference: Capture schema was designed around render diagnostics, not synchronized combat-truth observability.
red_gates.observed_fact: Submitted AOVs supply ten image layers but no contact, action-phase, physics-tick, camera, or truth-marker channel. Eight panels are pixel-distinct, yet they are all Reveal timestamps and do not visibly establish eight semantic phase anchors or opponent contact.
red_gates.prohibited_fallback: Do not equate nonduplicate frames, filenames, or inferred timing with explicit phase/truth/contact evidence.
red_gates.smallest_future_falsifying_experiment: Emit synchronized immutable phase/tick/camera/truth/contact channels for eight named anchors and prove one-to-one hash/time alignment with beauty/AOV panels.

red_gates.likely_cause_inference: The PVP-specific validator hard-codes its local 512/2048 schema and never compares output dimensions to permanent renderer thresholds.
red_gates.observed_fact: Permanent thresholds require 2048 px individual views and 4096 px sheets; current config/renderer enforce 512 px tiles and 2048 px sheets. Both validators still print PASS_CONFIG_ONLY. Public PR comment discussion_r3584107740 identifies the same gap.
red_gates.prohibited_fallback: Do not lower permanent thresholds or report config-only PASS as pixel acceptance.
red_gates.smallest_future_falsifying_experiment: Add a cross-contract test that feeds current config and must fail before render; then render at the permanent dimensions without changing thresholds.

red_gates.likely_cause_inference: Source motion intentionally remains unpromoted after failed raw/human gates, while a visual-only camera weapon response preserves a placeholder presentation.
red_gates.observed_fact: At exact HEAD, src/main.rs:738-746 returns c0_reference_skin for both actors; src/main.rs:604-608 and src/renderer.rs:16-30 place the sword with a camera-relative first-person transform. No exact-HEAD live-runtime visual capture exists.
red_gates.prohibited_fallback: No bind/reference pose, camera-relative independent sword, diagnostic candidate capture, or telemetry-only proof may satisfy live runtime.
red_gates.smallest_future_falsifying_experiment: Run native gameplay with admitted articulated poses and a pose-derived weapon, then bind live joint/weapon transforms, replay truth, and full pixels at one exact revision.

red_gates.likely_cause_inference: Owner evidence was copied from a CI artifact whose revision is contextual in its directory/artifact name but omitted from payload schemas.
red_gates.observed_fact: Tracked d84950f RTX receipt and owner llvmpipe directory have different index/config hashes and crop/grip results. Owner index/capture JSON does not self-embed a git revision. Current HEAD has no visual index.
red_gates.prohibited_fallback: Never merge metrics, pixels, hashes, renderer adapters, or review decisions across d84950f, e3c5-context, 6d65265, or exact HEAD.
red_gates.smallest_future_falsifying_experiment: Make capture/index self-record exact commit/tree/config/tool hashes; verify the uploaded artifact bytes against the local index and reject any missing binding.

red_gates.likely_cause_inference: Path overlay has no visible accumulated samples in those strips, and index completion bookkeeping does not distinguish emitted placeholders from accepted layer evidence.
red_gates.observed_fact: Four Strike/Block first-person accumulated_weapon_path files are byte-identical to their beauty files. Owner index declares remaining_layers=[wireframe] even though 54 wireframe PNGs exist. File existence therefore does not prove layer semantics/completeness.
red_gates.prohibited_fallback: Do not count byte-identical beauty as a verified path overlay or count nominal wireframe filenames as accepted completeness.
red_gates.smallest_future_falsifying_experiment: Inject a deterministic known weapon trajectory and assert visible/path-pixel differences from beauty plus semantic layer checks; reconcile index completeness only after per-layer validation.

expected_non_repository_outputs: new design-unit receipts and revision-bound candidate evidence; implementation filenames are intentionally not guessed before that unit
red_gates.likely_cause_inference: Offline proposals/checkpoints do not yet satisfy planted-contact, interaction-conditioned, human-readability, and live transition requirements.
red_gates.observed_fact: ARDY ledger verdict is fail with 0 structural passes for each action in both iterations. MotionBricks admission says released_checkpoint_requires_interaction_conditioning_extension and playable_proof=false.
red_gates.prohibited_fallback: Do not relabel offline kinematic proposals, released checkpoint output, or contract-only validators as admitted runtime motion.
